items.content: F01-R design repair. Read evidence/F01/design.md and the full independent FAIL review at reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md (commit 7563a1237). Revise the F01 design to resolve EVERY blocker and important contradiction. Required: implementable lower-crate inversion seam for leases covering pooled AND per-session MCP calls plus background tasks; exact proposed crate/module placement and dependency direction; complete single-executor coordinator concurrency protocol with full reason priority lattice, begin_and_wait semantics, exact deadline update rules and exactly-once cleanup/termination; remove ReloadHandoff as drain-blocking lease or explicitly make it administrative; central provider-turn guard at actual common lifetime boundary, separate bounded startup recovery lease, enumerate all entry families; continuous quiescence epoch semantics; coordinator-owned forced-exit watchdog outcome and residue contract; only real cleanup APIs or explicit new API ownership; temporary-server reload disposition; accept-loop failure completion reporting. Amend WORK_GRAPH.json F02 owned paths if the chosen design requires MCP/neutral crate files, applying changes consistently to expansions and all_nodes. Write revisions under evidence/F01/ (update design.md and README.md, add revision_response.md mapping each review finding to a correction). Commit ONLY evidence/F01/** and WORK_GRAPH.json if amended. No source code. Artifact must cite exact source and review lines.

F01-V independent critical re-review. Review the revised F01 design and revision_response.md against every blocker/important finding in reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md, ACCEPTANCE_STANDARD A0/A1, current source, and any WORK_GRAPH ownership amendments. Verify all source citations and crate dependency feasibility. Verdict PASS only if no owner/lease gap, self-dependency, undefined concurrency, incomplete turn/MCP coverage, idle semantics contradiction, watchdog loophole, fake cleanup API, temporary reload omission, or accept-loop completion ambiguity remains. Write reviews/F01-architecture-re-review.md naming exact commit and actual model. Commit only that file.

Final plan-wide critique: audit the ENTIRE plan adversarially before it may finish. Read every completed node's artifact, especially each 'what_i_did_not_check' and every open question, and hunt for whole facets the plan never covered. For each gap, inject a new node; the plan cannot finish until they drain.

Read reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md in full and evidence/F01/design.md. Produce an exhaustive finding-by-finding blocker/contradiction matrix with exact review line citations and required correction criteria. Analysis only, do not edit files.

Inspect the actual source tree and cited design/review to derive an implementable lower-crate inversion seam for leases that covers pooled MCP calls, per-session MCP calls, and background tasks. Specify exact crate/module placement, APIs, dependency direction, and exact source line citations. Analysis only, no edits.

Enumerate every provider-turn and MCP execution entry family in the actual codebase, identify the true common lifetime boundary for a central provider-turn guard, and design a separate bounded startup recovery lease. Include exact source file:line evidence and gaps. Analysis only, no edits.

Design a complete single-executor coordinator concurrency protocol resolving the critique: state machine, full reason priority lattice, begin_and_wait semantics, concurrent callers, deadline rules, exactly-once cleanup and termination. Tie every rule to exact review and source lines. Analysis only, no edits.

Define continuous quiescence epoch semantics robust to zero/nonzero lease transitions, late arrivals, drain/reload races, and waiters. Identify exact integration points with source file:line citations and review lines. Analysis only, no edits.

Design coordinator-owned forced-exit watchdog outcome and residue contract, including ownership, arming/cancellation, deadlines, terminal outcomes, reporting, exactly-once behavior, and cleanup residue. Cite exact source and review lines. Analysis only, no edits.

Audit every cleanup/termination API named or implied by evidence/F01/design.md against real source. Identify real APIs versus inventions and, for missing ones, propose explicit new API ownership/module/signature. Include exact source and review line citations. Analysis only, no edits.

Resolve temporary-server reload disposition and accept-loop failure completion reporting. Inspect actual server/task lifecycle source, propose implementable semantics/APIs, and cite exact source and review lines. Analysis only, no edits.

Analyze ReloadHandoff semantics in design and source. Recommend removal as a drain-blocking lease or formal administrative treatment, with race analysis and exact source/review citations. Analysis only, no edits.

Inspect WORK_GRAPH.json, especially F02 owned_paths in expansions and all_nodes. Determine exact amendments required if the corrected seam introduces MCP or neutral-crate files. Report all duplicated locations and consistency invariants with line/path evidence. Analysis only, no edits.

i2.artifact_json: {"findings":"I2 is substantiated. Severity classification: it is an IMPORTANT finding, not one of the critique's three items under “Blocking findings” (review:174-195; review:10-28). However, it is an acceptance-gate blocker: the required F01 revision gate expressly requires reconciling watchdog behavior with I2, I5, I7, and A0 before acceptance (review:291-306, item 8). The design has a direct internal contradiction: it calls ShutdownCoordinator the only voluntary exit path and says direct process::exit outside it is a violation (design:140-144), but retains an OS-thread watchdog as the hard bound (design:165-178; wiring explicitly says it stays, design:188-195). The declared phase graph and I2 only allow Exited after Draining then CleaningUp, except Handoff (design:146-154, 257-258), while I7 authorizes watchdog preemption to skip cleanup (design:267-269). That forced direct exit therefore has no coherent state-machine outcome and cannot satisfy I2. I5 also budgets coordinator completion as drain deadline plus cleanup deadline, but merely says it is enforced by the OS-thread watchdog (design:263-265), without ensuring those budgets fit below the watchdog deadline. Runtime evidence confirms the risk: on SIGTERM the existing code starts an independent OS thread that sleeps 3 seconds then calls process::exit(0), before the handler's direct unregister-and-exit sequence (server.rs:1204-1223). A forced exit can occur while the future coordinator is still Draining and before CleaningUp, after which it cannot reliably log skipped cleanup. This conflicts with A0's requirement that normal exit paths use one bounded authority and that socket/debug socket/lock/hash/metadata/registry sidecars are live-and-coherent or fully removed (ACCEPTANCE_STANDARD.md:7-15). Therefore I7's 'watchdog preemption is the only allowed skip' exception is insufficient and contradicts A0.\n\nRequired correction criteria: (1) the coordinator, not the SIGTERM handler or a detached external thread, exclusively arms, resets/disarms, and records the watchdog; no independently reachable direct process::exit may remain; (2) model a distinct ForcedExit/ForcedTermination terminal outcome, never report it as successfully completed Exited, and make its reason/deadline/last completed cleanup step durable or synchronously observable before termination; (3) state an explicit hard-deadline inequality that budgets intake stop, drain/abandon, every A0-required cleanup step, and logging below the watchdog deadline, with a defined failure policy for an individual step; (4) tighten I7 so skipped steps cannot leave an A0 sidecar incoherent. Either all A0-required sidecars must complete before forced termination, or the design must define and test an approved recovery contract that still meets A0 rather than calling skipped cleanup success; (5) test the forced path at each preemption boundary, especially during Draining and each cleanup step, asserting no successful Exited transition, exact exit status, exact lifecycle record, no owned descendants, and the full socket/debug socket/lock/hash/metadata/registry residue contract. items.content: F01-R design repair. Read evidence/F01/design.md and the full independent FAIL review at reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md (commit 7563a1237). Revise the F01 design to resolve EVERY blocker and important contradiction. Required: implementable lower-crate inversion seam for leases covering pooled AND per-session MCP calls plus background tasks; exact proposed crate/module placement and dependency direction; complete single-executor coordinator concurrency protocol with full reason priority lattice, begin_and_wait semantics, exact deadline update rules and exactly-once cleanup/termination; remove ReloadHandoff as drain-blocking lease or explicitly make it administrative; central provider-turn guard at actual common lifetime boundary, separate bounded startup recovery lease, enumerate all entry families; continuous quiescence epoch semantics; coordinator-owned forced-exit watchdog outcome and residue contract; only real cleanup APIs or explicit new API ownership; temporary-server reload disposition; accept-loop failure completion reporting. Amend WORK_GRAPH.json F02 owned paths if the chosen design requires MCP/neutral crate files, applying changes consistently to expansions and all_nodes. Write revisions under evidence/F01/ (update design.md and README.md, add revision_response.md mapping each review finding to a correction). Commit ONLY evidence/F01/** and WORK_GRAPH.json if amended. No source code. Artifact must cite exact source and review lines.

F01-V independent critical re-review. Review the revised F01 design and revision_response.md against every blocker/important finding in reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md, ACCEPTANCE_STANDARD A0/A1, current source, and any WORK_GRAPH ownership amendments. Verify all source citations and crate dependency feasibility. Verdict PASS only if no owner/lease gap, self-dependency, undefined concurrency, incomplete turn/MCP coverage, idle semantics contradiction, watchdog loophole, fake cleanup API, temporary reload omission, or accept-loop completion ambiguity remains. Write reviews/F01-architecture-re-review.md naming exact commit and actual model. Commit only that file.

Final plan-wide critique: audit the ENTIRE plan adversarially before it may finish. Read every completed node's artifact, especially each 'what_i_did_not_check' and every open question, and hunt for whole facets the plan never covered. For each gap, inject a new node; the plan cannot finish until they drain.

Read reviews/F01-architecture-critique.md in full and evidence/F01/design.md. Produce an exhaustive finding-by-finding blocker/contradiction matrix with exact review line citations and required correction criteria. Analysis only, do not edit files.

Inspect the actual source tree and cited design/review to derive an implementable lower-crate inversion seam for leases that covers pooled MCP calls, per-session MCP calls, and background tasks. Specify exact crate/module placement, APIs, dependency direction, and exact source line citations. Analysis only, no edits.

Enumerate every provider-turn and MCP execution entry family in the actual codebase, identify the true common lifetime boundary for a central provider-turn guard, and design a separate bounded startup recovery lease. Include exact source file:line evidence and gaps. Analysis only, no edits.

Design a complete single-executor coordinator concurrency protocol resolving the critique: state machine, full reason priority lattice, begin_and_wait semantics, concurrent callers, deadline rules, exactly-once cleanup and termination. Tie every rule to exact review and source lines. Analysis only, no edits.

Define continuous quiescence epoch semantics robust to zero/nonzero lease transitions, late arrivals, drain/reload races, and waiters. Identify exact integration points with source file:line citations and review lines. Analysis only, no edits.

Design coordinator-owned forced-exit watchdog outcome and residue contract, including ownership, arming/cancellation, deadlines, terminal outcomes, reporting, exactly-once behavior, and cleanup residue. Cite exact source and review lines. Analysis only, no edits.

Audit every cleanup/termination API named or implied by evidence/F01/design.md against real source. Identify real APIs versus inventions and, for missing ones, propose explicit new API ownership/module/signature. Include exact source and review line citations. Analysis only, no edits.

Resolve temporary-server reload disposition and accept-loop failure completion reporting. Inspect actual server/task lifecycle source, propose implementable semantics/APIs, and cite exact source and review lines. Analysis only, no edits.

Analyze ReloadHandoff semantics in design and source. Recommend removal as a drain-blocking lease or formal administrative treatment, with race analysis and exact source/review citations. Analysis only, no edits.

Inspect WORK_GRAPH.json, especially F02 owned_paths in expansions and all_nodes. Determine exact amendments required if the corrected seam introduces MCP or neutral-crate files. Report all duplicated locations and consistency invariants with line/path evidence. Analysis only, no edits.

Independently audit evidence/F01/design.md claims against actual source and review lines. Find stale/wrong citations, unsupported APIs, dependency-direction contradictions, and missing entry paths. Produce correction list. Analysis only, no edits.

Derive a concrete acceptance checklist for the revised F01 artifacts and WORK_GRAPH consistency from the user request and independent FAIL review. Include exact required artifact sections, citation standards, commit scope checks, and validation commands. Analysis only, no edits.
